questions.q: A charged particle moves in a circular path under the influence of a magnetic field. The direction of the magnetic field is?
opts: (A) Perpendicular to the direction of motion
(C) Opposite to the direction of motion
(D) Same as the direction of motion
questions.explanation: The charged particle is moving in a circular path due to the magnetic force, which acts perpendicular to the direction of motion.
questions.topic: Motion of a Charged Particle in a Magnetic Field

questions.q: The magnetic field at the centre of a circular loop carrying current is?
opts: (B) Directly proportional to the radius of the loop
(C) Inversely proportional to the radius of the loop
(D) Inversely proportional to the square of the radius of the loop
questions.explanation: The magnetic field at the centre of a circular loop is zero because the field due to each element of the loop is cancelled by the field due to the diametrically opposite element.
questions.topic: Magnetic Field Due to Current-Carrying Circular Loop

questions.q: The magnetic induction due to a current-carrying long straight wire at a distance r is?
questions.explanation: The magnetic induction due to a current-carrying long straight wire at a distance r is given by the Biot-Savart law.
questions.topic: Magnetic Field Due to Current-Carrying Long Straight Wire

questions.q: A moving charge creates a magnetic field. What is the direction of the magnetic field at a point due to a positive charge moving with a velocity?
opts: (B) Opposite to the direction of motion
(C) Perpendicular to the direction of motion
(D) In the direction of the force
questions.explanation: The direction of the magnetic field at a point due to a positive charge moving with a velocity is perpendicular to the direction of motion.
questions.topic: Magnetic Field Due to a Moving Charge

questions.q: The force acting on a current-carrying conductor placed in a magnetic field is?
questions.explanation: The force acting on a current-carrying conductor placed in a magnetic field is given by F = qvB, where q is the charge, v is the velocity, and B is the magnetic field.
questions.topic: Force on a Current-Carrying Conductor in a Magnetic Field

questions.q: What is the effect of increasing the speed of the conductor on the force experienced by it in a magnetic field?
questions.explanation: Increasing the speed of the conductor increases the force experienced by it in a magnetic field.
questions.topic: Force on a Current-Carrying Conductor in a Magnetic Field

questions.q: A rectangular loop of wire carrying current is placed in a uniform magnetic field. Which part of the loop experiences the maximum force?
opts: (A) The side parallel to the magnetic field
(B) The side perpendicular to the magnetic field
(C) The side at an angle to the magnetic field
(D) The side opposite to the direction of the magnetic field
questions.explanation: The part of the loop that experiences the maximum force is the side perpendicular to the magnetic field.
questions.topic: Force on a Current-Carrying Conductor in a Magnetic Field

questions.q: A coil of wire is placed in a magnetic field. What is the effect of increasing the number of turns of the coil on the force experienced by it?
questions.explanation: Increasing the number of turns of the coil increases the force experienced by it.
questions.topic: Force on a Current-Carrying Conductor in a Magnetic Field

questions.q: A current-carrying wire is placed in a magnetic field. What is the effect of increasing the current in the wire on the force experienced by it?
questions.explanation: Increasing the current in the wire increases the force experienced by it.
questions.topic: Force on a Current-Carrying Conductor in a Magnetic Field

questions.q: A solenoid is placed in a magnetic field. What is the effect of increasing the current in the solenoid on the magnetic field produced by it?
questions.explanation: Increasing the current in the solenoid increases the magnetic field produced by it.
questions.topic: Magnetic Field Due to a Current-Carrying Solenoid

questions.q: A moving coil galvanometer is placed in a magnetic field. What is the effect of increasing the current in the coil on the deflection of the galvanometer?
questions.explanation: Increasing the current in the coil increases the deflection of the galvanometer.

questions.q: A current-carrying wire is placed in a magnetic field. What is the direction of the force acting on the wire?
opts: (A) Along the direction of the current
(B) Opposite to the direction of the current
(C) Perpendicular to the direction of the current
(D) In the direction of the magnetic field
questions.explanation: The direction of the force acting on the wire is perpendicular to the direction of the current.
questions.topic: Force on a Current-Carrying Conductor in a Magnetic Field

questions.q: A rectangular loop of wire carrying current is placed in a uniform magnetic field. What is the direction of the torque acting on the loop?
opts: (A) Along the axis of the loop
(B) Opposite to the axis of the loop
(C) Perpendicular to the axis of the loop
(D) In the direction of the magnetic field
questions.explanation: The direction of the torque acting on the loop is along the axis of the loop.
questions.topic: Torque on a Current-Carrying Conductor in a Magnetic Field

questions.q: A current-carrying solenoid is placed in a magnetic field. What is the effect of increasing the current in the solenoid on the magnetic field produced by it?
questions.explanation: Increasing the current in the solenoid increases the magnetic field produced by it.
questions.topic: Magnetic Field Due to a Current-Carrying Solenoid

questions.q: A moving coil galvanometer is placed in a magnetic field. What is the effect of increasing the magnetic field on the deflection of the galvanometer?
questions.explanation: Increasing the magnetic field increases the deflection of the galvanometer.

questions.q: A current-carrying wire is placed in a magnetic field. What is the effect of increasing the velocity of the wire on the force experienced by it?
questions.explanation: Increasing the velocity of the wire increases the force experienced by it.
questions.topic: Force on a Current-Carrying Conductor in a Magnetic Field

questions.q: A rectangular loop of wire carrying current is placed in a uniform magnetic field. What is the direction of the force acting on the loop?
opts: (A) Along the axis of the loop
(B) Opposite to the axis of the loop
(C) Perpendicular to the axis of the loop
(D) In the direction of the magnetic field
questions.explanation: The direction of the force acting on the loop is perpendicular to the axis of the loop.
questions.topic: Force on a Current-Carrying Conductor in a Magnetic Field

questions.q: A current-carrying solenoid is placed in a magnetic field. What is the effect of increasing the number of turns of the solenoid on the magnetic field produced by it?
questions.explanation: Increasing the number of turns of the solenoid increases the magnetic field produced by it.
questions.topic: Magnetic Field Due to a Current-Carrying Solenoid

questions.q: A moving coil galvanometer is placed in a magnetic field. What is the effect of increasing the current in the coil on the sensitivity of the galvanometer?
questions.explanation: Increasing the current in the coil increases the sensitivity of the galvanometer.
